message.content: @startuml actor User participant Frontend participant API database Database == Log in with token == User -> Frontend : Request token alt [Token found] Frontend -> API : Validate token activate API API -> Database : Find token activate Database Database --> API : Return matching user deactivate Database alt note right of API: Token not in database\nReturn error return "error" else API --> Frontend : Open main page end if else API -X>Frontend :Open loginpage deactivate API endif note over User,Database #DDF0FF#:#FFFFFF;Log in without token;[font=Arial][size =12];[/size][/font][Enter login information]\nAccept to be remembered \nPress \"log in\" \nRequest validation from frontend\nto api for validating credentials.\nIf successful add new session id/token into db record associated w/that account so next time they can just click remember me checkbox instead having re-enter their username/password again! Otherwise show an appropriate message indicating what went wrong during authentication process like invalid password entered etc.. Retry until correct info provided by client side application itself through UI components such forms fields where users input data directly onto them before submitting those values back server-side via HTTP POST requests made using AJAX calls initiated upon clicking submit buttons located within said form elements themselves which then trigger corresponding event handlers defined inside
